title: jacobaea vulgaris subsp. dunensis
intro: Jacobaea vulgaris subsp. dunensis, commonly known as St. John's-wort or Dun's St. John's-wort, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. It has been used in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Jacobaea vulgaris subsp. dunensis include its potential to alleviate symptoms of anxiety and depression, as well as its ability to protect against oxidative stress and cell damage. Jacobaea vulgaris subsp. dunensis is often prepared as a tea or tincture for its herbal uses, although it is essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using it as a treatment.
